behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR." Slot B: handoff across local modules (commit-diff calc + age filter + PR-state join). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a single-function test can miss cross-module mismatch (e.g., PR state keyed by wrong branch ref) that marks stale incorrectly. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require "live" repo/API or real subprocess/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector service with in-process git-state fixture provider and PR-state fixture provider; assert stale true only when all three predicates are jointly satisfied and false when any predicate is absent.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning ... when the current branch is stale." Slot B: handoff between bootstrap hook, staleness detector result, and warning renderer. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss wiring failures where bootstrap never calls detector or drops branch context fields. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess signal in text; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior does not require real shell/session process behavior, only setup wiring outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer in-process with a stubbed stale branch state and capture emitted notices; verify one warning is emitted and includes branch name, age, and next action text.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state ... deterministically." Slot B: single local function logic boundary. Slot C: if lowered below Unit (not possible), deterministic mapping bugs would be untested; at Unit they are directly exposed. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly scopes to a "local ... function" with deterministic mapping, not module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven pure-function test over branch-state inputs to assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and stable results across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "against live repo and PR state" and "in rendered output." Slot B: real-infra boundary (real git/repo state + real PR API/network + command rendering path). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, mocked repo/PR adapters can pass while real git ancestry or live PR lookup/auth/timeouts produce wrong status text. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because no AI/ML model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo with actual git history and real PR state source (or real staging equivalent API), then assert rendered status switches correctly between `clean` and `STALE` scenarios.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives." Slot B: handoff between PR-status interpretation and stale classifier gating. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, tests can miss mismatch where detector receives normalized PR states differently (`open`/`merged`) and still flags stale. Integration-brake: (a) behavior does not require live network/API; (b) no AI/ML output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because text does not require "live" PR backend; correctness target is state-handling logic across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Compose detector + PR-state adapter with fixture cases for `open` and `merged`; assert stale-no-pr is never emitted when commits exist but PR linkage is present in either terminal/open state.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change." Slot B: real-infra boundary via real git history transformations (rebase/squash semantics). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, synthetic commit fixtures may miss true git DAG/merge-base behavior and still misclassify post-rewrite branches as stale. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior depends on VCS mechanics, not model output.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, create branch commits, perform rebase and squash-merge flows, then run staleness detection and verify branch is no longer marked unPRed stale after history rewrite/merge completion.
